findings.reachability: Any authenticated WordPress user (subscriber, customer) who can obtain a valid nonce for the action 'flexible_shipping_get_label' can reach this handler via admin-post.php; check_admin_referer() only prevents CSRF, it does not enforce role or capability.
findings.justification: The handler calls check_admin_referer() but never calls current_user_can() with any shipping-related capability, so any logged-in user who holds a nonce (e.g. exposed on the page they normally see) can download any shipment label by supplying an arbitrary shipment_id.

detection_pattern.section_name: Access Control: admin_post_ handler without capability check
detection_pattern.description: Finds admin_post_ action registrations; files lacking current_user_can() nearby perform privileged actions with only CSRF protection, allowing any authenticated user to trigger them.

findings.reachability: Triggered by any authenticated user reaching dispatch_label_if_requested(); exploitable if label_data['file_name'] from the shipment record contains newline characters.
findings.justification: header('Content-Disposition: attachment; filename=' . $label_data['file_name']) passes the filename from stored shipment data directly into a header() call without sanitization, quoting, or newline stripping; a stored newline in the filename allows injecting additional HTTP response headers.

detection_pattern.description: Finds header() calls that concatenate a variable into a Content-Disposition filename without basename() or sanitize_file_name(); newlines in the variable enable HTTP response splitting / header injection.

findings.reachability: Triggered when label_action=open is set and label_format is not 'html'; exploitable if the stored label_format value in the shipment record contains newline characters.
findings.justification: 'application/' . $label_data['label_format'] is concatenated directly into a Content-Type header via header() without any validation or newline stripping, enabling HTTP header injection if label_format contains CRLF.

detection_pattern.section_name: Header Injection: unsanitized value in Content-Type header
detection_pattern.description: Finds header() calls that build a Content-Type value by concatenating a variable; without CRLF stripping or allowlisting the value can inject arbitrary HTTP headers.

findings.reachability: Reached by any authenticated user with a valid nonce when label_action=open and the shipment's label_format is 'html'; the stored label content is echoed verbatim with Content-Type: text/html.
findings.justification: echo $label_data['content'] outputs the raw stored label content with no escaping; when prepare_content_type() returns 'text/html' (label_format === 'html'), the browser renders arbitrary HTML/JS embedded in the label, constituting stored XSS.

detection_pattern.section_name: XSS: unescaped echo of stored content field
detection_pattern.description: Finds echo statements that output a 'content' array element without escaping; when served with a text/html content type this allows stored XSS from attacker-influenced stored data.
